display.purpose: Route titles and major route headers.

headline.purpose: Selected detail headers and major section titles.

title.purpose: Panel headings and compact section headers.

body.purpose: Descriptions, helper text, and operational copy.

label.purpose: Field labels, status metadata, and compact UI labels.

code.purpose: IDs, paths, commands, and token-like data.

shadows.name: subtle-surface
shadows.purpose: Small structural elevation for buttons, cards, and quiet panels.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Dialog and trace row entry transitions.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Global reduced-motion override for animations and transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Strong neutral product action for utilitarian screens.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; min-height: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.20 0.006 70); background: oklch(0.18 0.006 70); color: oklch(0.97 0.006 86); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 16px; font: 500 14px/20px Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.18 0.006 70 / 0.15); transition: background-color 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.13 0.006 70); border-color: oklch(0.13 0.006 70); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.25); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default page and toolbar action with bordered paper surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; min-height: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--surface); color: var(--text); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 16px; font: 500 14px/20px Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); transition: background-color 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--bg); border-color: rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.30); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.25); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Accent Button
components.description: Activation button for workflow launch or equivalent high-signal action.
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { display: inline-flex; min-height: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; border: 1px solid var(--brand-orange); background: var(--brand-orange); color: oklch(0.99 0.004 86); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 16px; font: 500 14px/20px Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.20); transition: background-color 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-btn-accent:hover { background: var(--brand-orange-bright); border-color: var(--brand-orange-bright); } .ds-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.25); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Field
components.description: Stable page search input with paper fill and orange focus ring.
components.css: .ds-search-field { height: 44px; width: 280px; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--surface); color: var(--text); border-radius: 8px; padding: 0 12px; font: 400 14px/20px Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; outline: none; transition: box-shadow 200ms ease, border-color 200ms ease; } .ds-search-field:hover { border-color: rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.30); } .ds-search-field:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.20); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Semantic status chip with soft background and uppercase text.
components.css: .ds-status-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border: 1px solid rgb(var(--status-success-rgb) / 0.25); background: rgb(var(--status-success-soft-rgb)); color: rgb(var(--status-success-text-rgb)); border-radius: 999px; padding: 2px 8px; font: 700 10.88px/1 Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.055em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Surface Card
components.description: Operational card surface with warm border and subtle structural shadow.
components.html:
Agent library

3 agents, 11 capabilities, 2 workflow assignments
components.css: .ds-surface-card { border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--surface); color: var(--text); border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05); font-family: Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-surface-card h3 { margin: 0; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-surface-card p { margin: 4px 0 0; color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 14px; line-height: 24px; font-weight: 400; }

components.name: Navigation Item
components.description: Compact sidebar navigation row with muted default state and soft signal hover.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { width: 100%; display: flex; min-height: 40px; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; border: 0; background: transparent; color: var(--text-muted); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; font: 500 14px/20px Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background-color 200ms ease, color 200ms ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgb(var(--brand-orange-soft-rgb) / 0.55); color: var(--brand-orange-strong); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgb(var(--brand-orange-rgb) / 0.25); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Dialog Panel
components.description: Focused modal surface with warm border, strong lift, and explicit title.
components.html:
Create agent

Name, provider, and purpose are enough to save a restricted agent.
components.css: .ds-dialog-panel { max-width: 520px; border: 1px solid var(--border); background: var(--surface); color: var(--text); border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 25px 50px -12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.25); font-family: Outfit, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-dialog-panel h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 20px; line-height: 28px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-dialog-panel p { margin: 8px 0 0; color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; font-weight: 450; }

narrative.overview:
The AcornOps Management Console is a product interface for operators and developers who need to inspect workspace state, trace execution, and make deliberate changes without losing context. The visual system should feel like a ledger: warm, orderly, precise, and built for repeated reading under pressure.

The system is restrained by design. Warm neutral surfaces carry most of the experience, orange appears only when the interface needs a signal, and dense operational surfaces stay readable through hierarchy, spacing, borders, and stable component behavior. It rejects decorative SaaS patterns, route-breaking cleverness, opaque local state, direct backend calls outside the control-plane client boundary, and visual treatments that compete with diagnostics or trace readability.
keyCharacteristics: Warm neutral shell with one controlled orange signal.
Dense but readable pages for repeated operational scanning.
Familiar product controls with predictable keyboard and focus behavior.
Borders and tonal layers before shadow.
Motion only when it explains state.

rules.name: The Single Signal Rule
rules.body: Orange is the only primary accent. If a screen needs another loud hue, it must be semantic status or data visualization.

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: Backgrounds and borders stay warm and quiet. Do not introduce cool slate, blue-gray, or generic dashboard neutrals.

rules.name: The Activation Rule
rules.body: Orange filled buttons are for workflow launch or activation. Routine product actions use neutral primary or secondary buttons.

rules.name: The No Display Drama Rule
rules.body: Display type is for route identity, not marketing. Do not add oversized hero typography inside operational pages.

rules.name: The Weight-First Rule
rules.body: Use font weight and muted color before adding new type sizes. New ad-hoc sizes should be rare.

rules.name: The Border Before Shadow Rule
rules.body: Use borders and tonal layers before adding elevation. If a shadow is visible before the border is readable, the shadow is too strong.

rules.name: The No Glass Rule
rules.body: Blur-backed panels and glass cards are prohibited for this product.

dos: Do keep the console control-plane-backed: parse and normalize API payloads at the client boundary before UI use.
Do protect route stability: workspace, Kubernetes cluster, VM, and tab links must remain shareable.
Do prioritize trace readability and operational scan speed over local cleverness.
Do use predictable controls, visible labels, visible focus states, and stable dimensions.
Do reserve orange for actions, selected state, focus, and meaningful state.
Do keep dense pages readable through borders, spacing rhythm, typography, and progressive disclosure.
donts: Don't use opaque local state abstractions that hide control-plane state.
Don't make direct backend calls outside the control-plane client boundary.
Don't use visual treatments that compete with diagnostics or trace readability.
Don't use orange as a generic accent; reserve orange filled buttons for workflow launch or activation.
Don't use glassmorphism, gradient text, decorative bokeh, or ornamental orbs.
Don't use border-left or border-right wider than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't make status, role, permission, or health depend on color alone.
